commit_scope: Switch Form tissue reads to Rust default and rebalance source_inventory across sibling kernels

commands: cd form/form-kernel-ts && npm ci && npm run check

git diff --check -- Makefile README.md cli/README.md mcp-server/README.md skills/coherence-network/SKILL.md docs/shared/cell-voice-protocol.md docs/shared/agent-start-packet.md docs/coherence-substrate/form-language.md docs/system_audit/commit_evidence_2026-06-07_form_cell_voice_tissue.json docs/system_audit/commit_evidence_2026-06-07_rust_default_source_inventory_rebalance.json form/form-kernel-rust/src/main.rs form/form-kernel-ts/src/kernel.ts form/scripts/pyfkb-run.sh form/form-stdlib/carrier-tissue.fk form/form-stdlib/queries/carrier-tissue-census.fk form/form-stdlib/queries/cell-voice-tissue.fk form/form-stdlib/tests/carrier-tissue-kernel-query-band.fk form/form-stdlib/tests/cell-voice-tissue-band.fk
local_validation.summary: Rust is now the default carrier for Makefile tissue reads. source_inventory is implemented in Rust and TypeScript to match the existing Go primitive, and the full carrier census query agrees across Go/Rust/TypeScript: [65202, false, 5329, 2062708, 739, 3865, 92, 540, 93, 501, 577, 14, 24, 819, 845, 27, 61, 604, 21].

known_failed_attempts.output: Initial check failed with sh: tsc: command not found because node_modules was absent in this worktree. Resolved with npm ci && npm run check.

Initial full census parity diverged only on renderer punctuation: Go/Rust rendered the leading string label bare, TypeScript quoted it. Replaced the label with existing numeric CT-TAG-CENSUS so the query is structurally tagged and parity passes.

Rust-default smoke failed with undefined name print; explicit Go returned null. This is an unsupported Python-BMF built-in sample, not a Rust-default regression. Supported expression smoke 7 + 3 returns 10 on Rust and Go.

ci_validation.reason: No commit, push, or PR was created in this turn.

phase_gate.reason: Local proof passed, but CI and production deploy validation remain pending until the work is committed, pushed, and shipped.

evidence_refs: Makefile tissue targets build and run form-kernel-rust by default.
form/form-kernel-rust/src/main.rs binds source_inventory(root, suffix, skip-dir-names) with sorted source walking and line counts.
form/form-kernel-ts/src/kernel.ts binds source_inventory with the same sorted walk and line-count row shape.
form/form-stdlib/carrier-tissue.fk uses CT-TAG-CENSUS for the census structure so sibling parity is structural, not renderer-dependent.
form/scripts/pyfkb-run.sh defaults to Rust and uses Rust for source-compile prelude preparation.
docs/shared/cell-voice-protocol.md, docs/shared/agent-start-packet.md, docs/coherence-substrate/form-language.md, and generated README surfaces no longer teach the source walk as Go-owned.

e2e_validation.expected_behavior_delta: The active Form tissue read path defaults to Rust without making source_inventory a Rust-only or Go-only primitive; Go, Rust, and TypeScript agree on the full source census, and pyfkb-run uses Rust by default for supported Python-BMF expression execution.
test_flows: cd form && ./validate.sh form-stdlib/carrier-tissue.fk form-stdlib/queries/carrier-tissue-census.fk returns 1 ok, 0 divergent.
make carrier-vitality returns [carrier-vitality, 65202, false, 5329, 739, 138, 4590, 3865, 92, 540, 93, 5945, lens/load: name repeated external carriers into cells].
make cell-voice-tissue returns cell-voice-tissue for 5329 files and 2062708 LOC.
cd form && ./scripts/pyfkb-run.sh <(printf '7 + 3\n') returns 10; --kernel go returns 10 for the same sample.

known_runtime_context.default_kernel_rotation: Rust is the current default for Form tissue Make targets. No kernel is primary; the default can rotate again when sibling parity proof is present.
known_runtime_context.remaining_named_asymmetry: The Go HTTP front-door listener and some JIT probes remain Go-authored route/runtime tissue from earlier work. This evidence only resolves the source_inventory and tissue-read default mismatch.
known_runtime_context.python_bmf_print_gap: The Python-BMF harness does not model print as a built-in today. Expression and function samples are the supported smoke path; print remains a named language-feature gap rather than a Rust default blocker.
